claims.text: The apm-policy.yml file is located at <org>/.github/apm-policy.yml and is auto-discovered by apm install and apm audit --ci --policy org.
Policy enforcement has two modes: install time (preflight gate) and CI time (audit gate).
The enforcement field has three values: block, warn, and off.
MCP transport policy accepts http and stdio but blocks sse and streamable-http by default.
The apm install --no-policy flag and APM_POLICY_DISABLE=1 environment variable skip policy enforcement at install time.
Child repo policies can only tighten parent org policies, never relax them.
Allow list merge rule: child sees only entries present in both parent and child (intersection).
Deny list merge rule: child adds to parent's deny list (union).
Inheritance chains support up to 5 levels: enterprise hub -> org policy -> team policy -> repo override.
Policy violations at install time halt the operation with non-zero exit code before files are written.
apm audit --ci --policy org produces SARIF output that GitHub Code Scanning renders inline on PR diffs.
Policy file can specify allow and deny lists for dependency sources using org globs and package patterns.
The max_depth field merge rule is min(parent, child) - whichever is smaller wins.
The trust_transitive field merge rule is parent AND child - both must allow it.
The enforcement field can escalate from off to warn to block, but a child can never move enforcement backward.
